title: orobanche californica subsp. californica
intro: Orobanche californica subsp. californica, also known as the California broomrape, is a parasitic plant that derives its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants, typically those in the families Solanaceae and Polemoniaceae. Traditionally, Orobanche californica subsp. californica has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and anti-catarrhal properties. In herbal preparations, Orobanche californica subsp. californica is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
